behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is purely within one function's pattern-matching logic applied to a static file — no module handoff is involved. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network/subprocess required; a single file read plus regex/pattern check is sufficient. LLM-DEP: No model output involved. MULTI-STEP: No. REJECTION-GATE: Considered Integration (in case the lint rule must coordinate with a SKILL.md parser module), but the behavior specifies a lint check on a template file, which is a single boundary — the check either fires on the template text or it doesn't.
behaviors.test_description: Read a synthetic prompt-template string containing a fragment verbatim-copied from a fixture SKILL.md. Invoke the lint function directly. Assert it returns a failure/error identifying the leakage. Also assert a clean template (no copied fragment) passes. No file system writes, no subprocess, no LLM.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation/fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure only appears when the interpolation/fixture pipeline assembles the final prompt — leakage could be introduced by the harness itself during variable substitution, not just present in the raw template. A Unit test against the raw template would miss this. REAL-INFRA: No real HTTP or OS events needed; the harness assembly runs in-process. LLM-DEP: The test checks the assembled string, not model output. MULTI-STEP: No. REJECTION-GATE: Considered Unit (the assembled string is just text), but the behavior explicitly requires the real harness pipeline to run so that interpolation-introduced leakage is caught — making the module boundary load-bearing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real test harness with representative fixtures/interpolation inputs. Invoke the full prompt-assembly pipeline end-to-end. Capture the assembled prompt string. Assert no phase-instruction fragment from SKILL.md appears in it. No LLM call needed; use a stub for any model interface.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from the plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure crosses two boundaries: (1) the file-loading pipeline must correctly wire skill context into the prompt, and (2) the model must produce output that demonstrates the skill context actually governed its behavior. REAL-INFRA: The skill files are real filesystem artifacts loaded at runtime — an in-process stub for model output would hide the distinction between 'model responded to skill context' and 'model echoed hard-coded rules.' LLM-DEP: 'Validates behavior from that context' is inherently LLM-dependent — a deterministic stub always passes regardless of whether skill context is present or absent. MULTI-STEP: Single inference call. REJECTION-GATE: Considered Integration (just verify the loaded context appears in the assembled prompt), but 'validates behavior' requires the model to actually respond in a context-driven way — a structural check on the prompt alone doesn't confirm the model uses the context.
behaviors.test_description: Load the real plugin/skill files. Assemble the prompt using the production loader (no manual context injection). Send the assembled prompt to the real LLM. Assert that the model's response reflects skill-specific behavioral characteristics that are only derivable from the SKILL.md content, not from any hard-coded rule in the test.

behaviors.description: If the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails with the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure only manifests as a model behavior change — without real LLM output, any stub would return a predetermined response and the test would always pass or always fail regardless of SKILL.md state. REAL-INFRA: No OS/subprocess needed beyond file reads. LLM-DEP: This is the canonical regression-detection case: correctness depends entirely on whether the real model's output degrades when skill context is removed. A deterministic mock cannot reproduce this. MULTI-STEP: Single inference call, not a multi-agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered (verify the prompt changes when SKILL.md is degraded), but the behavior explicitly requires the behavioral test to fail — meaning model output must degrade, not just the prompt.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize over two SKILL.md states: (a) intact, (b) target section removed/degraded. For each, run the same user-style prompt through the real LLM. Assert that state (a) produces output satisfying the behavioral assertion, and state (b) does not — i.e., the test is sensitive to skill content regression.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is an ordering issue in the pipeline — capture must happen before parsing. A Unit test of the capture function alone cannot verify it runs before the parser in the real execution path. The bug only appears when the two modules are wired together. REAL-INFRA: The artifact can be an in-process buffer or temp file; no real subprocess or external network needed. The failure mode (parsing runs before capture) is reproducible in-process. LLM-DEP: The model can be replaced with a deterministic stub returning a known raw string; the test verifies pipeline ordering, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: No agentic steps. REJECTION-GATE: Considered System (artifact written to real disk), but the ordering invariant is fully observable with an in-process temp file or captured stream — real OS behavior adds nothing to catching this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a deterministic stub that returns a known raw string (including intentionally malformed JSON). Wire the capture module and JSON parser in the production execution order. Assert the artifact contains the raw string before parsing is attempted. Assert parsing errors do not suppress or overwrite the artifact. No real LLM call needed.

behaviors.description: Assertions verify structured behavior (for example field placement or schema shape), not mere keyword presence that could come from prompt echoing.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is in the assertion strategy — an assertion that passes on a keyword-echoing response is indistinguishable from a correct response at the Unit level. To expose the distinction, you must run the assertion logic against two response types: (a) correct structured output and (b) a response that contains the right keywords but wrong structure. These two response types can be constructed deterministically without a real LLM. REAL-INFRA: No real network/OS needed. LLM-DEP: The responses used to exercise the assertion can be deterministic strings — the failure mode (assertion passes on echo response) does not require LLM non-determinism to reproduce. MULTI-STEP: No. REJECTION-GATE: Considered Agentic (need real model to produce realistic echo responses), but the tautology failure can be exposed with a hand-crafted echo response that contains the target keywords without the required schema shape — no LLM variation needed.
behaviors.test_description: Construct two deterministic response strings: (a) correct structured output meeting the schema, (b) a keyword-echo response containing the target terms but with wrong/missing field structure. Run the assertion under test against both. Assert that (a) passes and (b) fails — proving the assertion is schema-sensitive, not keyword-sensitive. No real LLM call needed.
